resources:

Please enter password. Please enter a valid email/phone number. Open navigation drawer Close navigation drawer LOGOUT HOME Home Application Version PROFILE PRIVACY POLICY OFFLINE SYNC Community Health Worker CHW Households Search Add Household Household No No of people Members Registered Filters %1$d/%2$d Investigation INVESTIGATION Prescription PRESCRIPTION COUNSELLING Counselling SUBMIT Household Name / No Diagnoses Confirm Diagnoses Patient Status Referred Edit Diagnoses Edit Status Patient Category General Edit Category Presenting Complaints Type your notes Clinical Notes Systemic Examinations Other None Assessment Household Members FINISH REGISTRATION ADD NEW MEMBER Household Details House No Village Landmark Household Head Mobile Number %1$s - %2$s - %3$s Patient Medical Review
%1$.0f mmHg %1$.0f BPM Date of Birth Age (in years) DD/MM/YYYY --Select-- Enter the Age Enter the value Confirm Yesterday Today SKIP Hrs Mins AM PM Pulse Don\'t have ID? Generate ID - -- : - # Next Please enter a valid value back More home (Red Alert) Scroll View Household Registration Cancel Member Registration Submit Start Assessment Date of Birth Years Months Weeks Days Age - No Household found. Household Name / No Member Deceased M F ICCM TB National ID Bio Data Name Gender %1$s Months %1$s Weeks %1$s Years Result Patient\'s Summary Could not be fetched Referred for Further Assessment Done General Danger Signs MUAC Cough Fever Breathing Rate Dispensed RDT Test Diarrhoea Signs Label Write your observation Notes Yes No Nearest PHU Referred to Was the child actually taken to the Clinic ? Next visit date Presumptive TB No TB Signs & Symptoms Symptoms No Symptoms Selected Symptoms Selected Select Symptoms OK Please check your internet connectivity. Incorrect email or password Close Error Alert Retry Session Expired! Please login again. You does not have internet to connect our system. Do you wish to continue? Role Email Phone No Assigned Health Facility SPICE Suite Access Back %1$s %2$s Registration Status Pending Finished Apply\ Filter (%1d) Villages Phone number should starts with %1$s or Edit Household Edit Member Select Member OKAY %1$s M %1$s W %1$s Y Household registration \n completed successfully. Household No : %1$s Patient ID Select Flow ANC PNC Childhood Visit %1$s - %2$s Location service disabled! Please turn on your Location service to get your current location to proceed Search Patient Advanced Search First Name Last Name Mobile Number %1d Patients No patients found to upload. Search by National ID Referral Ticket Cost Next Medical Review Summary Pregnancy Summary Pregnancy History Diagnosis Disease Category Disease Condition Prescriptions Medical Supplies Normal Moderate Malnutrition Severe Malnutrition %1$s (%2$s) Severe Dehydration Pneumonia BPM Malaria %1$d (≥ %2$d days) HH ID Date of Onset Date of Review Different Login! There is some unsynchronized data associated with the last user (%1$s). Kindly sync data. Sync Status Something went wrong during upload. Please try again later %1$d record yet to sync Instructions Measuring MUAC Checking for Oedema Chest in-drawing Patient On Treatment No Referral / No Treatment Required Select Ask the mother to remove any clothing that covers the child’s left arm. Identify the midpoint of the child’s left upper arm. This can be done by taking a piece of string (or the tape itself), place one end on the tip of the child’s shoulder and the other on the elbow Now bend the string up in a loop to double it so the point at the elbow is placed together with the point on the shoulder with a loop hanging down – the end of the straightened loop indicates the midpoint. Straighten the child’s arm and wrap the tape around the arm at the midpoint. Make sure the tape is flat around the skin. Inspect the tension of the tape on the child’s arm. Make sure the tape has the proper tension and is not too tight so that the skin is compressed or too loose so that the tape does not contact the skin all the way round the arm. When the tape is in the correct position on the arm with correct tension, pay attention to the color of MUAC Use your thumbs to press gently on the top of both feet at the same time and say “1001 1002 and 1003”. Lift the fingers and observe if a ‘pit’ remains The child has oedema if a pit remains in both feet when you lift your thumbs The child has chest in-drawing if the lower chest wall goes IN when the child breathes IN. Chest in-drawing is not visible when the child breathes out. For chest in-drawing to be present, it must be clearly visible and present at every breath. 34 weeks(Premature) Gestational Age 1780gms weeks(Low birth weight) "Last Menstrual Period " Estimated Delivery Date Refer Patient Treatment Next visit date reload Select Review Date Selected Referral Date Referral Reason Referred To Referred By Date Of Delivery Date Type Labour/Delivery Date of Delivery Date of Labour onset Delivery Type Delivery At No of Neonates Time of Delivery Time of Labour Onset Delivery By Delivery Status Please enter valid input Select Date Number Hour Minutes Member registration \n completed successfully. Patient ID : %1$s Result - Mother Result - Neonatal ANC Visit PNC Visit If you leave this screen, your entered data will not be saved. Add New Member Birth History Breathing Problem at birth Clinical Summary & Vital Signs Weight (in Kgs) WAZ Temperature (in C) Temperature Respiration Rate Rate minute Height (in cms) WHZ Immunisation Status Repeat (if >60) Breast feeding Exclusive Breast feeding Vitamin A for mother Mother Signs/Symptoms observed General Condition Of Mother Risk Factors State Of The Perineum Episotomy Tear TT Dose taken so far Status Neonate Neonate Outcome Male Female State Of Baby Abnormal Birth Weight (in Kg) Signs/Symptoms observed State of the Baby APGAR Score 1 Deg 2 Deg 3 Deg 4 Deg Labour/Delivery Medical Review Labour/Delivery Search medication and add Medication Name Frequency Prescribed days Quantity No. of fetus Pregnancy Details Gravida Height (in cm) Weight (in kg) BMI : Systolic(hhHg) Diastolic(mmhg) + Parity "Please enter weight between 10 to 400" "Please enter height between 50 to 300" "Please enter Gestational age below 60" Patient Blood Group Diastolic pressure should be above 30 Pulse should be above 35 Systolic pressure should be above 30 Examination Clinician Name The review has been successfully submitted. Review Completed Finish Add Weight Blood Pressure Add Blood Pressure Enter the weight Diastolic pressure should be above 30 Pulse should be above 35 Systolic pressure should be above 30 Diastolic pressure should be lesser than 300 Pulse should be lesser than 300 Systolic pressure should be lesser than 300 Systolic pressure should be greater than Diastolic pressure Call Result Unsuccessful Successful On Treatment Recovered Data Range From To Unreachable Wrong Number please select from date Medical Review HH Visit No Patient found. %1$d Patient Reason Start Date HH Name Calls Made Indicator 1 MINUTE 5 MINUTE 10 MINUTE A-Activity P-Pulse G-Grimace A-Appearance R-Respiration Total save Select Disease Category Select Disease Condition Add Diagnoses Jaundice Gene Expert 1.Jaundice -- child June Breathing problems at birth Birth History Clinical Summary & Vital Signs /minute Day Morning Night (%1$d tablets per day- %2$d days) (%1$s tablets per day- %2$d days) Amoxicillin 250mg Zinc Tablet (with 1 litre of water) ORS Packet ACT-6 %1$d %2$s Tablet Tablets No Dosage Recommended for this age group Are you sure want to logout ? Entered value should contain only alphabets Up to Date Not Up to Date Upcoming start Offline data Your offline data sync has successfully completed! Failed to sync offline data now. Please try again later! Offline data has started to sync Start offline sync now? Offline Sync Network Not Available Please check your internet connection and try again. Minimum %1$s characters required Entered value should be %1$s to %2$s Entered value should be greater than %1$s Entered value should be lesser than %1$s Household Please select a valid site %1$s Tomorrow Pregnancy Summary Fundal Height (in cms) Fetal Heart Rate(BPM) Obstetric Examination Contact Number Mother Neonate Anc Saved Successfully Moderate Dehydration My Patients Something went wrong Last Synced At: Cut-offs for fast breathing Instructions to calculate breathing rate 2 to 11 Months 1 to 5 Years 50 or more Breaths per minute 40 or more Make sure that your respiratory timer is ready. Make sure the child is calm. If the child is sleeping, do not wake her/him - Ask caregiver to gently remove clothing from the chest and tummy. Make sure you are able to see the child’s chest; observe the child’s breathing; and see when the chest moves in and out One breath in and out makes one breath count. When ready to count, press the centre of your timer (Note that the timer beeps every second for 60 seconds; will alarm in 30 seconds and then in 60 seconds before stopping beeping) Keep counting until the timer stops beeping Fast Breathing dd/mm/yyyy Please enter a valid mobile number (Zinc - %1$s), (ORS - %2$s) Physical Examinations Congenital Detect Cord Examination Healing satisfactorily Poor healing Infected
Filter Refer To Referred Reason Type your comments Name/Number Refer %1$s (%2$s) / %3$s %4$s Dispense Renew All Clear Signature Signature is required Sign and Confirm Prescription week Month kg mmHg Zinc ORS (%1$s - %2$s) Remove Medication Medical Review History cms beats per minute Does mother have a delivery kit? Pregnancy History (Past obstetric history) No Referral History found %1$s(%2$s) Select diagnosis found on the patient Something went wrong. Please try again later View Discontinued Medication Hide Discontinued Medication No data found. Edit Confirmation Are you sure you want to delete it? Prescribed Since "Please enter value between 1 to %1$d"
